ForeignAddress.AddressLine1Txt: LVL 3 JD STORY BUILDING UNIV OF
LVL 3 JD STORY BUILDING UNIV OF

IRS990.MissionDesc: THE UNIVERSITY OF QUEENSLAND (THE "UNIVERSITY") WAS ESTABLISHED BY AN ACT OF PARLIAMENT IN 1909 TO DISSEMINATE KNOWLEDGE AND PROMOTE SCHOLARSHIP THROUGH THE OPERATION OF A UNIVERSITY IN BRISBANE, AUSTRALIA. TODAY, THE UNIVERSITY IS A PREMIER RESEARCH AND TEACHING INSTITUTION IN AUSTRALIA, WITH APPROXIMATELY 56,000 STUDENTS AND 7,000 FULL-TIME EQUIVALENT EMPLOYEES, INCLUDING APPROXIMATELY 2,600 FACULTY MEMBERS. THE UNIVERSITY HAS DEPARTMENTS OR "FACULTIES" IN THE AREAS OF ARTS, BUSINESS, ECONOMICS AND LAW, ENGINEERING, ARCHITECTURE AND INFORMATION TECHNOLOGY, HEALTH SCIENCES, SCIENCE, AND SOCIAL AND BEHAVIORAL SCIENCES. IN ADDITION, THE UNIVERSITY HAS EIGHT OTHER INSTITUTES, INCLUDING THE INSTITUTE FOR MOLECULAR BIOSCIENCE, QUEENSLAND BRAIN INSTITUTE, GLOBAL CHANGE INSTITUTE AND THE QUEENSLAND ALLIANCE FOR AGRICULTURE AND FOOD INNOVATION. IT OPERATES THROUGH OVER 50 SITES THROUGHOUT QUEENSLAND AND HAS ADDITIONAL INVOLVEMENT WITH APPROXIMATELY 120 OTHER CENTERS AND INSTITUTES.
IRS990.Desc: EDUCATION, SPONSORED RESEARCH & PROJECTS AND OTHER INSTITUTIONAL ACTIVITIES.

ForeignAddress.AddressLine1Txt: LVL 3 JD STORY BUILD UNIV OF QLD

Form990PartVIISectionAGrp.TitleTxt: CFO Part Year & COO Part Year
EXEC. DEAN FAC. OF HMAN & SS

Form990PartVIISectionAGrp.PersonNm: ADJ Prof Dr Sally Pitkin AO
ADJ ASSOC PROF DIMITY Dornan AO

SupplementalInformationDetail.FormAndLineReferenceDesc: SCHEDULE D, PART III:
SupplementalInformationDetail.ExplanationTxt: THE UNIVERSITY WARMLY WELCOMES GIFTS OF INNOVATIVE ARTWORK ACROSS A RANGE OF MEDIA. THE ART COLLECTION FOSTERS NEW ARTISTIC INQUIRY; WHILST THE RD MILNS ANTIQUITIES MUSEUM IS NOW THE FOREMOST COLLECTION OF CLASSICAL MEDITERRANEAN ANTIQUITIES IN QUEENSLAND. A HIGHLY ACCESSIBLE TEACHING MUSEUM FOR BOTH THE GENERAL PUBLIC AND UNIVERSITY-LEVEL STUDENTS AND RESEARCHERS.

ForeignIndividualsGrantsGrp.RegionTxt: Russia and the Newly Independent States
Russia and the Newly Independent States
Russia and the Newly Independent States

SupplementalInformationDetail.FormAndLineReferenceDesc: SCHEDULE F, PART I, LINE 1:
SupplementalInformationDetail.ExplanationTxt: ALL DISBURSEMENTS FROM UQ ARE MADE IN THE FURTHERANCE OF UQ'S EXEMPT PURPOSE AND REVIEWED AND APPROVED BY AUTHORIZED FINANCIAL DELEGATES. UQ ALSO HAS A DESIGNATED CONTRACTS & GRANTS TEAM WHO MONITOR AND REPORT TO INTERNAL AND EXTERNAL BODIES ON GRANTS, INCLUDING THE EXPENDITURE INCURRED AND WHETHER THE DEALINGS ARE IN ACCORDANCE WITH THE CONTRACT/AGREED TERMS.

SupplementalInformationDetail.FormAndLineReferenceDesc: SCHEDULE F, PART I, II, III:
SupplementalInformationDetail.ExplanationTxt: EXPENDITURES ARE RECORDED ON AN ACCRUAL BASIS. AMOUNTS REPORTED ARE ESTIMATED BASED ON THE BEST AVAILABLE DATA. "UNIVERSITY ACTIVITIES" INCLUDE ACADEMIC SUPPORT, INSTITUTIONAL SUPPORT, TEACHING, RESEARCH AND OTHER ACADEMIC ACTIVITY, SERVICE CENTERS AND STUDENT SERVICES.

SupplementalInformationDetail.FormAndLineReferenceDesc: SCH F, PART II, COLUMN D:
SupplementalInformationDetail.ExplanationTxt: SPONSORED RESEARCH

SupplementalInformationDetail.FormAndLineReferenceDesc: SCHEDULE I, PART I, LINE 2:
SupplementalInformationDetail.ExplanationTxt: ALL DISBURSEMENTS FROM UQ ARE MADE IN THE FURTHERANCE OF UQ'S EXEMPT PURPOSE AND REVIEWED AND APPROVED BY AUTHORIZED FINANCIAL DELEGATES. UQ ALSO HAS A DESIGNATED CONTRACTS & GRANTS TEAM WHO MONITOR AND REPORT TO INTERNAL AND EXTERNAL BODIES ON GRANTS, INCLUDING THE EXPENDITURE INCURRED AND WHETHER THE DEALINGS ARE IN ACCORDANCE WITH THE CONTRACT/AGREED TERMS.

SupplementalInformationDetail.FormAndLineReferenceDesc: SCHEDULE I, PART II, COLUMN H, LINE 1:
SupplementalInformationDetail.ExplanationTxt: SURVEY RESEARCH SERVICES

SupplementalInformationDetail.FormAndLineReferenceDesc: SCHEDULE I, PART II, COLUMN H, LINE 4:
SupplementalInformationDetail.ExplanationTxt: JUVENILE DIABETES RESEARCH

SupplementalInformationDetail.FormAndLineReferenceDesc: SCHEDULE I, PART II, COLUMN H, LINE 6:
SupplementalInformationDetail.ExplanationTxt: SAMPLING METHODOLOGIES

SupplementalInformationDetail.FormAndLineReferenceDesc: SCHEDULE I, PART II, COLUMN H, LINE 8:
SupplementalInformationDetail.ExplanationTxt: ADVANCED MODELLING FOR MARINE RESEARCH

SupplementalInformationDetail.FormAndLineReferenceDesc: SCHEDULE I, PART II, COLUMN H, LINE 9:
SupplementalInformationDetail.ExplanationTxt: STUDYING SENSORY NETWORKS IN BRAIN

RltdOrgOfficerTrstKeyEmplGrp.TitleTxt: EXEC. DEAN FAC. OF HMAN & SS
CFO Part Year & COO Part Year

SupplementalInformationDetail.FormAndLineReferenceDesc: SCHEDULE J, PART I, LINE 1:
SupplementalInformationDetail.ExplanationTxt: The University travel policy prohibits first class travel. Business Class travel is permitted for Senate members within Australia and TODKE for international travel greater than 7hrs in length.

SupplementalInformationDetail.FormAndLineReferenceDesc: SCHEDULE J, PART I, LINE 5A:
SupplementalInformationDetail.ExplanationTxt: KPIS (KEY PERFORMANCE INDICATORS) ARE SET BY THE REMUNERATION COMMITTEE WITH REVENUES OF THE UNIVERSITY ONE OF THE FACTORS.

SupplementalInformationDetail.FormAndLineReferenceDesc: SCHEDULE J, PART I, LINE 6A:
SupplementalInformationDetail.ExplanationTxt: KPIS (KEY PERFORMANCE INDICATORS) ARE SET BY THE REMUNERATION COMMITTEE WITH NET EARNINGS OF THE UNIVERSITY ONE OF THE FACTORS CONSIDERED AS PART OF THE BONUS PAYMENT.

SupplementalInformationDetail.FormAndLineReferenceDesc: SCHEDULE M, PART I, COLUMN (B):
SupplementalInformationDetail.ExplanationTxt: THE ORGANIZATION IS REPORTING THE NUMBER OF CONTRIBUTIONS RECEIVED.

SupplementalInformationDetail.FormAndLineReferenceDesc: FORM 990, PART I, LINE 1:
SupplementalInformationDetail.ExplanationTxt: UQ IS ONE OF AUSTRALIA'S LEADING RESEARCH AND TEACHING INSTITUTIONS. FOR MORE THAN A CENTURY, UQ HAS EDUCATED AND WORKED WITH OUTSTANDING PEOPLE TO DELIVER KNOWLEDGE LEADERSHIP FOR A BETTER WORLD.

SupplementalInformationDetail.FormAndLineReferenceDesc: FORM 990, PART VI, SECTION A, LINE 7A:
SupplementalInformationDetail.ExplanationTxt: THE QUEENSLAND STATE PARLIAMENT APPOINTS 8 MEMBERS TO THE GOVERNING BODY.

SupplementalInformationDetail.FormAndLineReferenceDesc: FORM 990, PART VI, SECTION B, LINE 11B:
SupplementalInformationDetail.ExplanationTxt: THE FORM IS PREPARED BY AN EXTERNAL ACCOUNTING FIRM BASED ON INFORMATION COLLATED BY THE UNIVERSITY'S TAX TEAM. THE FORM IS REVIEWED BY THE ASSOCIATE DIRECTOR, CORPORATE FINANCE AND APPROVED BY THE CHIEF FINANCIAL OFFICER.

SupplementalInformationDetail.FormAndLineReferenceDesc: FORM 990, PART VI, SECTION B, LINE 12C:
SupplementalInformationDetail.ExplanationTxt: DISCLOSURE OF OUTSIDES INTEREST IS MANDATORY AND EACH COMMITTEE MEETING REQUIRES NOTING OF THE DECLARATIONS AND THE OPPORTUNITY FOR ANY CONFLICTS TO BE ADVISED IN RELATION TO THE MATTERS TO BE DISCUSSED AT THE MEETING.

SupplementalInformationDetail.FormAndLineReferenceDesc: FORM 990, PART VI, SECTION B, LINES 15A AND 15B:
SupplementalInformationDetail.ExplanationTxt: THE GOVERNING BODY HAS A REMUNERATION COMMITTEE WHICH REVIEWS AND DETERMINES THE REMUNERATION PACKAGE AND ANNUAL PERFORMANCE BONUS OF THE VICE-CHANCELLOR, BASED ON AN ANNUAL PERFORMANCE REVIEW AND BENCHMARKED AGAINST COMPARABLE ORGANISATIONS. THE COMMITTEE ALSO APPROVES THE REMUNERATION CHANGES AND PERFORMANCE BONUS PAYMENTS FOR THE VICE-CHANCELLOR'S DIRECT REPORTS.

SupplementalInformationDetail.FormAndLineReferenceDesc: FORM 990, PART VI, SECTION C, LINE 19:
SupplementalInformationDetail.ExplanationTxt: THE UNIVERSITY OF QUEENSLAND ACT IS ACCESSIBLE ON THE QLD STATE GOVERNMENT'S LEGISLATION WEBSITE: HTTPS://WWW.LEGISLATION.QLD.GOV.AU/

SupplementalInformationDetail.FormAndLineReferenceDesc: FORM 990, PART VI, SECTION C, LINE 20
SupplementalInformationDetail.ExplanationTxt: AS TELEPHONE NUMBER THE FULL AS INTERNATIONAL NUMBER IS +61 7 334 63424. THE NUMBER IS ABBREVIATED FOR ELECTRONIC FILING PURPOSES ON FORM 990, ITEM E AND PART VI, SECTION C, LINE 20, AS (173)346-3424.

SupplementalInformationDetail.FormAndLineReferenceDesc: FORM 990, PART X:
SupplementalInformationDetail.ExplanationTxt: THE BALANCE IS IN AUSTRALIAN DOLLAR CURRENCY.

SupplementalInformationDetail.FormAndLineReferenceDesc: FORM 990, PART XI, LINE 9:
SupplementalInformationDetail.ExplanationTxt: COST OF GOODS SOLD $ 7,643,241 Fair Value adjustment $ 135,175,000 Other Changes $ 82,758,691 NET ASSETS AND FUND BALANCE $ 225,576,932
